exercises.title: What does MDC add to logs?
exercises.expected_solution:
19:05:23 [REQ-ABC] [USR-001] INFO  - Payment started for amount=999.0

%X{key}: MDC key lookup — reads the thread-local MDC map.
%X{requestId} → 'REQ-ABC'
%X{userId} → 'USR-001'
Both values appear in the log line WITHOUT explicit passing to log.info().

test_cases.expected:
19:05:23 [REQ-ABC] [USR-001] INFO  - Payment started for amount=999.0

%X{key}: MDC key lookup — reads the thread-local MDC map.
%X{requestId} → 'REQ-ABC'
%X{userId} → 'USR-001'
Both values appear in the log line WITHOUT explicit passing to log.info().

hints: %X{key}: Logback MDC lookup syntax
MDC is thread-local: all log statements in the thread include MDC values
No explicit passing: MDC automatically available in all log calls

exercises.title: Complete the LoggingContextFilter
exercises.instructions: Fill in the MDC filter pattern.

blanks.hint: MDC.clear() — removes all entries from this thread's MDC

hints: MDC: org.slf4j.MDC — thread-local key-value store for logging context
clear() in finally: thread pool reuse requires this — never skip
OncePerRequestFilter: ensures filter runs exactly once per request

exercises.title: Configure JSON structured logging
exercises.instructions: Write pom.xml dependency + logback-spring.xml for JSON structured output using logstash-logback-encoder. Show sample JSON output.
exercises.expected_solution:
<!-- pom.xml: -->
<!-- <dependency> -->
<!--   <groupId>net.logstash.logback</groupId> -->
<!--   <artifactId>logstash-logback-encoder</artifactId> -->
<!--   <version>7.4</version> -->
<!-- </dependency> -->

<!-- logback-spring.xml: -->
<!-- <configuration> -->
<!--   <springProfile name='prod'> -->
<!--     <appender name='JSON' class='ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender'> -->
<!--       <encoder class='net.logstash.logback.encoder.LogstashEncoder'/> -->
<!--     </appender> -->
<!--     <root level='INFO'> <appender-ref ref='JSON'/> </root> -->
<!--   </springProfile> -->
<!--   <springProfile name='dev'> -->
<!--     <appender name='STDOUT' class='ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender'> -->
<!--       <encoder><pattern>%d [%X{traceId}] %-5level - %msg%n</pattern></encoder> -->
<!--     </appender> -->
<!--     <root level='DEBUG'> <appender-ref ref='STDOUT'/> </root> -->
<!--   </springProfile> -->
<!-- </configuration> -->

// SAMPLE JSON OUTPUT (prod profile):
// {"@timestamp":"2024-06-15T19:05:23.456Z","level":"INFO","message":"Payment processed",
//  "logger":"com.razorpay.PaymentService","traceId":"abc123","userId":"USR-001",
//  "requestId":"REQ-001","paymentId":"PAY-001","amount":999.0}

public class StructuredLoggingDemo {
    public static void main(String[] a) {
        System.out.println("JSON output: all MDC fields + message in one searchable JSON line");
    }
}
hints: LogstashEncoder: outputs JSON with MDC fields automatically included
springProfile: different format for dev (readable) vs prod (JSON)
LogstashEncoder includes ALL MDC entries as JSON fields automatically

exercises.title: Complete LoggingContextFilter with all business context
exercises.instructions:
Write complete LoggingContextFilter for Razorpay:
1. Sets: requestId (UUID), userId (from header), merchantId (from header), path
2. Clears MDC in finally
3. What happens if MDC is NOT cleared?
hints: UUID for requestId: generate if client doesn't provide X-Request-Id
durationMs in finally: measures total request time automatically
MDC.clear() without putting: alternative to clearing individual keys

exercises.title: Log levels: which level for each event?
exercises.instructions:
Write 5 log statements at correct levels for Razorpay:
1. Payment successful: log the outcome
2. DB connection pool at 90% capacity: degraded warning
3. Visa API returned error 12 (declined): business failure
4. Circuit breaker opened: resilience event
5. Inside validateCard() method entry: debug
hints: INFO: successful business events the team wants to count
WARN: degradation that may need attention later
ERROR: failures that need attention now
DEBUG: never show in production (disabled by logging.level config)

exercises.title: Test log output with Mockito
exercises.instructions:
Write a test verifying specific log messages are output:
1. Use ch.qos.logback.classic.Logger + ListAppender
2. Verify: paymentService.processPayment() logs INFO with 'Payment processed'
3. Verify: failed payment logs ERROR with 'Payment failed'
hints: ListAppender: captures log events in a list for assertion
addAppender before test, detachAppender after: clean isolation
getFormattedMessage(): fully formatted string with parameters substituted

exercises.title: Loki log query patterns
exercises.instructions:
Write 5 Loki queries (with comments) for Razorpay's structured logs:
1. All ERROR logs for payment-service
2. All slow payments (latencyMs > 1000)
3. All logs for a specific traceId
4. Error rate for gateway=visa in last 5 minutes
5. All logs for a specific merchantId
hints: LogQL: {labels} | json | fieldFilter
|= 'string': full-text search (before parsing)
rate(): turns log counts into a rate metric for Grafana

exercises.title: Structured payment audit log
exercises.instructions: Write PaymentAuditLogger: structured INFO logs for every payment with key business fields as structured parameters. Include: paymentId, amount, gateway, merchantId, status, durationMs. Show how these become queryable JSON fields.
hints: Parameterized logging: each {} becomes a queryable JSON field in structured log
MDC fields (userId, requestId) are added automatically by LogstashEncoder
Loki can filter by any JSON field: enables powerful cross-merchant analysis

exercises.title: MDC in async operations (CompletableFuture)
exercises.instructions:
MDC is thread-local — it doesn't propagate to CompletableFuture threads. Write AsyncMDCService:
1. Copy MDC context before going async
2. Restore in the async thread
3. Clear in async thread's finally
4. Test: MDC values available in async thread logs
hints: getCopyOfContextMap(): snapshot of current MDC (safe to pass across threads)
setContextMap() in async thread: restores the captured MDC
MDC.clear() in async finally: async threads return to pool too

exercises.title: What happens without MDC.clear()?
exercises.instructions:
```
Thread pool has 3 threads: T1, T2, T3.
Request 1 (userId=USR-001): processed by T1 → MDC set, but NOT cleared
Request 1 completes: T1 returned to pool (MDC still has userId=USR-001)
Request 2 (userId=USR-002): processed by T1 (pool reused T1!)
```
What appears in Request 2's logs for userId? What is the security/correctness implication?
exercises.expected_solution:
Request 2's logs show userId=USR-001 (wrong!).

Because:
  T1 processed Request 1, MDC set to userId=USR-001.
  MDC.clear() NOT called → MDC remains with userId=USR-001.
  T1 returned to pool.
  Request 2 arrives, pool assigns T1.
  Request 2 does NOT set MDC (filter never runs cleanly).
  All Request 2 log statements: userId=USR-001 (from previous request!)

Implications:
  1. CORRECTNESS: logs for Request 2 show wrong user
     → Debugging: engineer investigates wrong user's activity
     → Monitoring: metrics attributed to wrong user
  2. SECURITY/COMPLIANCE: audit logs show USR-001 for USR-002's activity
     → Regulatory: compliance audit fails (GDPR, PCI-DSS require accurate audit logs)
     → Billing: usage attributed to wrong merchant
  3. DATA CONTAMINATION: all MDC fields leak (requestId, merchantId, path)

Fix: ALWAYS MDC.clear() in the finally block of the filter.

test_cases.expected:
Request 2's logs show userId=USR-001 (wrong!).

Because:
  T1 processed Request 1, MDC set to userId=USR-001.
  MDC.clear() NOT called → MDC remains with userId=USR-001.
  T1 returned to pool.
  Request 2 arrives, pool assigns T1.
  Request 2 does NOT set MDC (filter never runs cleanly).
  All Request 2 log statements: userId=USR-001 (from previous request!)

Implications:
  1. CORRECTNESS: logs for Request 2 show wrong user
     → Debugging: engineer investigates wrong user's activity
     → Monitoring: metrics attributed to wrong user
  2. SECURITY/COMPLIANCE: audit logs show USR-001 for USR-002's activity
     → Regulatory: compliance audit fails (GDPR, PCI-DSS require accurate audit logs)
     → Billing: usage attributed to wrong merchant
  3. DATA CONTAMINATION: all MDC fields leak (requestId, merchantId, path)

Fix: ALWAYS MDC.clear() in the finally block of the filter.

hints: Thread pool: threads are reused across requests
MDC is thread-local: survives thread reuse
PCI-DSS: payment industry requires accurate audit logs — context leak is a compliance violation

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay structured logging implementation
exercises.instructions:
Build complete structured logging:
1. LoggingContextFilter: requestId, userId, merchantId, path
2. PaymentService: INFO on success, ERROR on failure with structured fields
3. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): INFO log on success, ERROR log on failure
hints: ListAppender captures log events for test assertion
Level.INFO vs Level.ERROR: exact match assertion
MDC.clear() in filter finally: tested indirectly via context isolation

exercises.title: Security: what NOT to log
exercises.instructions:
Write a code review showing what NOT to log and the correct alternatives:
1. Card number: log.info('Charging card {}', cardNumber)
2. API key: log.debug('Using API key {}', apiKey)
3. Full request body including PAN
4. Password in authentication: log.info('Authenticating {}', password)
Provide the secure version of each.
hints: PCI-DSS: cardholder data (full card number, CVV) must NEVER appear in logs
Mask before logging: last 4 digits only for card numbers
API key: first 8 chars are safe (the 'rzp_live_' prefix is public)

exercises.title: Full Zerodha trading audit trail with structured logs
exercises.instructions:
Build ZerodhaAuditLogger for SEBI compliance:
1. MDC filter: orderId, tradeType, symbol, userId
2. Structured log for order submission, execution, rejection, cancellation
3. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): order submitted logs INFO, rejection logs ERROR
4. Each log line must be parseable for SEBI audit
hints: SEBI audit: every order state change must be logged with timestamp, orderId, reason
MDC.remove vs MDC.clear: remove specific keys when not in a request context
ListAppender test: captures log events without network/file I/O

exercises.title: Log-based alerting via Loki + Grafana rules
exercises.instructions:
Write the Loki alerting queries and corresponding Grafana alert rules for Razorpay:
1. Alert: error rate > 1% in 5 minutes
2. Alert: any SEBI-critical log level=ERROR with source=orderExecution
3. Alert: MDC context leak (wrong userId appearing)
4. Show how Grafana Loki alerts complement Prometheus metric alerts
hints: Loki rate(): same as Prometheus rate() but on log counts
Level filter: {level='ERROR'} works on label; | json | level='ERROR' works on JSON field
Complement: Prometheus for SLO, Loki for debugging why SLO was breached

exercises.title: Complete M20 observability — all three pillars
exercises.instructions:
Build the complete Razorpay observability stack:
1. Metrics: Counter + Timer (T20.1)
2. Trace: custom span (T20.2)
3. Logs: MDC filter + structured INFO/ERROR (T20.3)
4. All three active in one payment method
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): metrics, tracing, and logging all work
hints: All three pillars in one method: timer.record wraps span which wraps log statements
MDC.remove in finally: clean up without clearing all MDC (partial clear)
Three tests: one per pillar — verify each independently
